assertions.text: Calls RegisterBackgroundWorker from _PG_init guarded by process_shared_preload_libraries_in_progress
Sets bgw_flags = BGWORKER_SHMEM_ACCESS | BGWORKER_BACKEND_DATABASE_CONNECTION (and explicitly NOT BGWORKER_CLASS_PARALLEL)
Picks bgw_start_time = BgWorkerStart_RecoveryFinished (not PostmasterStart)
Names a finite bgw_restart_time AND notes that proc_exit(0) retires the slot regardless of bgw_restart_time
Main loop uses WaitLatch with WL_LATCH_SET | WL_TIMEOUT | WL_EXIT_ON_PM_DEATH (all three flags named)
Signal handlers (SIGHUP=SignalHandlerForConfigReload, SIGTERM=die) installed BEFORE BackgroundWorkerUnblockSignals()
Explicitly forbids sleep()/usleep() in the worker body
Calls BackgroundWorkerInitializeConnection exactly once, AFTER unblocking signals, with the BGWORKER_BACKEND_DATABASE_CONNECTION flag justification
Wraps SQL/SPI work in StartTransactionCommand / CommitTransactionCommand pair
CHECK_FOR_INTERRUPTS() called inside the loop body
Points at source/src/test/modules/worker_spi/ as canonical example
Names PG_WAIT_EXTENSION (or equivalent custom wait event) as the WaitLatch event-class argument
Saves previous hook into a static planner_hook_type variable before installing own
Inside the callback, calls prev_planner_hook if non-NULL, else standard_planner
Shows the planner_hook_type prototype with ALL FIVE parameters (Query *, const char *, int, ParamListInfo, ExplainState *es)
Names _PG_init as the install site, invoked by the dynamic loader (dfmgr.c) when the library is loaded
States there is NO _PG_fini — libraries are never unloaded once loaded
DROP EXTENSION removes SQL catalog bindings but does NOT unload the .so / undo the hook
Distinguishes shared_preload_libraries vs session_preload_libraries vs local_preload_libraries (load timing)
Lists at least 4 other chainable hooks (e.g. ProcessUtility_hook, ExecutorStart_hook, ExecutorEnd_hook, emit_log_hook, shmem_request_hook, shmem_startup_hook)
Mentions that the work (audit log of plan tree) goes AFTER the chained call so we see the final plan

evals.question_short: Why die() for SIGTERM and SignalHandlerForConfigReload for SIGHUP

assertions.text: States die() sets ProcDiePending / InterruptPending and SetLatch — does NOT call proc_exit from the handler
Names ProcessInterrupts() (via CHECK_FOR_INTERRUPTS) as where the actual exit work runs, OUTSIDE signal-handler context
Identifies async-signal-safety as the root reason — palloc / LWLocks / file I/O / ereport are NOT signal-safe
Notes proc_exit(0) directly from handler would skip AbortCurrentTransaction() — locks / snapshots / resowner state left dangling
Notes proc_exit(0) return code = 'clean exit, do not restart' per bgworker contract (BGW_NEVER_RESTART-equivalent behavior on exit-0 regardless of bgw_restart_time)
SignalHandlerForConfigReload sets ConfigReloadPending + SetLatch (two-line body)
Main loop, not the handler, calls ProcessConfigFile(PGC_SIGHUP) after observing ConfigReloadPending
General rule named: signal handlers may only flip a flag + set latch + (sometimes) record sender pid; nothing else
Cites a file:line for die() (postgres.c) or SignalHandlerForConfigReload (interrupt.c) or worker_spi.c

notes: iter-1 with_skill miss (eval 2 assertion 3: 5-param planner_hook prototype) is closed by Edit 1 — SKILL.md now explicitly notes 'five parameters with ExplainState *es as the trailing argument' with file:line cite to source/src/include/optimizer/planner.h:28-32. with_skill goes from 29/30 → 30/30 (+1, +3.3pp). Baseline is unchanged at 17/30 because the prompts and general-PG-knowledge cohort are identical. Other applied edits (3: no _PG_fini block, 4: signal-handler rationale, 5: bgw_extra layout, 2/local_preload) harden against regression on the same assertion set but do not flip any iter-2 grade — they would lift score on prompts not in this iteration's set (e.g. 'why is my hook still active after DROP EXTENSION', 'how do I pack data into bgw_extra', 'local_preload_libraries vs session_preload').
